verdict.answer_first: B states it immediately: "For a single instance, default to a plain in-memory cache." A also does: "start with a plain in-memory cache," but B is shorter.
verdict.decisiveness: B commits cleanly: "in-memory unless you need persistence..." A also commits, but adds more caveats and a follow-up question.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on right now because it names concrete cache libraries: "Java: Caffeine", "Go: ristretto", "Node: lru-cache".
